Title: Multi-level engineering facilitates the production of phenylpropanoid compounds in tomato

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in tomato fruit pericarp/ripe fruit under the fruit-specific E8 promoter; strongly induced at 3–4 days after breaker and analyzed at 7–10 days after breaker
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (GEO: GSE61014); ChIP-seq (GEO: GSE62462); metabolomics/LC-MS; isotope labeling ([14C]-glucose, [13C]-glucose)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Phenylpropanoid compounds including flavonols, hydroxycinnamates/chlorogenic acid, anthocyanins, resveratrol derivatives, and isoflavones
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Tomato fruit pericarp/flesh and whole fruit
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A general metabolic-engineering tool for tomato fruit biofortification; can be stacked with TFs (Del/Ros1) or structural genes (VvStSy, LjIFS) to strongly elevate diverse phenylpropanoids by increasing both pathway demand and precursor/energy supply
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage (breaker to 10 days post-breaker); fruit-specific E8 promoter context
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Unlike Del/Ros1, AtMYB12 reprograms primary metabolism in addition to activating phenylpropanoid genes; constitutive 35S expression in other contexts gave more modest flavonol gains and reduced growth in tobacco, suggesting context dependence
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fruit-specific expression of AtMYB12 in tomato activates genes of both primary metabolism and secondary phenylpropanoid metabolism. 2. AtMYB12 directly binds promoters of SlENO and SlDAHPS as well as flavonoid genes, increasing carbon flux through glycolysis, the OPPP and shikimate pathway. 3. This multi-level control greatly boosts accumulation of flavonols and hydroxycinnamates and enables very high production of engineered resveratrol and isoflavones when stacked with additional genes.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive expression can reduce growth in tobacco; high stilbene production reduced flavonol accumulation due to pathway competition; no specific tomato yield penalty reported here
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in tomato; RNA-seq; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling; [14C]/[13C] isotope feeding and flux analysis; genetic crosses
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strong accumulation of multiple phenylpropanoid products by increasing both precursor supply and flavonoid-pathway gene expression
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyphenolic phytonutrients/bioactives with antioxidant and health-related value; includes compounds relevant to plant defense and potential human health benefits

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Phenylpropanoid compounds via aromatic amino acid supply
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Shikimate pathway supplying phenylalanine for phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Entry step into shikimate/aromatic amino acid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses phosphoenolpyruvate and erythrose-4-phosphate to form 3-deoxy-D-arabino-heptulosonate 7-phosphate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for aromatic amino acids and downstream phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key determinant of carbon flow into the shikimate pathway
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Upregulated in AtMYB12 tomato fruit; elevated in WT peel where SlMYB12 is expressed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq; qRT-PCR correlation in peel/flesh
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased expression is associated with enhanced aromatic amino acid supply and elevated phenylpropanoid accumulation in AtMYB12 fruit
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful upstream target for increasing precursor supply to aromatic amino acid-derived specialized metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct promoter target of both heterologous AtMYB12 and endogenous SlMYB12
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlDAHPS transcript is elevated in AtMYB12 tomato fruit. 2. Its promoter is directly bound by AtMYB12 and also by SlMYB12 in peel. 3. Upregulation supports increased carbon flow into shikimate-derived phenylpropanoid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native tomato promoter; activated by AtMYB12/SlMYB12

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Phenylpropanoid compounds via precursor and energy supply
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Glycolysis linked to shikimate and phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool and energy-related flux toward shikimate-derived phenylpropanoids
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream primary metabolism supplying upstream precursor
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key link between primary metabolism and aromatic amino acid/specialized metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Upregulated in AtMYB12 tomato fruit; elevated in WT peel where SlMYB12 is expressed
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; ChIP-qPCR; ChIP-seq; qRT-PCR correlation in peel/flesh
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased expression is associated with enhanced glycolytic flux and provision of phosphoenolpyruvate for shikimate/phenylpropanoid production
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate supply-side engineering gene to enhance carbon flux toward aromatic specialized metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct promoter target of both AtMYB12 and SlMYB12
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlENO is transcriptionally activated in AtMYB12 fruit. 2. AtMYB12 directly binds the SlENO promoter, and SlMYB12 binds the same motif in peel. 3. Its upregulation is linked to higher glycolytic flux supporting phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native tomato promoter; activated by AtMYB12/SlMYB12

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Stilbene biosynthesis branch from phenylpropanoid/flavonoid precursor metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Stilbene branch competing with chalcone/flavonoid formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Uses p-coumaroyl-CoA and malonyl-CoA to form resveratrol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Acts as decisive branch-switch enzyme for stilbene production in engineered tomato
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Expressed in tomato fruit in transgenic lines; original 35S:VvStSy line analyzed and crossed with E8:AtMYB12
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression; genetic crossing with AtMYB12; LC-MS quantification of resveratrol derivatives
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables resveratrol accumulation in tomato, which is strongly enhanced by co-expression with AtMYB12
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful structural gene for high-resveratrol tomato when combined with AtMYB12
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Co-expression with AtMYB12 increased total resveratrol compounds to 5–6 mg g^-1 DW; high stilbene production suppressed the high flavonol phenotype, indicating strong competition with CHS
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; comparison of 35S-alone versus co-expression with fruit-expressed AtMYB12
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VvStSy expression alone produced about 0.5 mg g^-1 DW trans-resveratrol in tomato fruit. 2. Co-expression with AtMYB12 raised total resveratrol and derivatives to 5–6 mg g^-1 DW. 3. Stilbene synthesis strongly competed with flavonoid synthesis, reducing flavonol accumulation in the stacked line.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced flavonol accumulation due to competition with chalcone synthase branch

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Isoflavone biosynthesis branch from flavonoid pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Isoflavone branch competing with F3H for naringenin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts flavanone substrate naringenin into isoflavone skeleton leading to genistein/genistin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Acts as decisive branch-introducing enzyme for engineered isoflavone production
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Fruit-specific transgene expression in tomato under E8 promoter
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression; genetic crossing with AtMYB12 and are mutant; LC-MS quantification of isoflavones
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables low genistin accumulation alone and much higher isoflavone production when combined with AtMYB12 and reduced F3H activity
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising gene for high-isoflavone tomato when stacked with AtMYB12 and branch rerouting via reduced F3H
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: LjIFS alone produced low genistin; with AtMYB12 increased to ~11 mg g^-1 DW; with AtMYB12 are-/- total isoflavones reached ~78 mg g^-1 DW
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit-specific E8 expression; genetic background with are mutant alters flux
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LjIFS introduced an isoflavone branch into tomato fruit and produced genistin. 2. AtMYB12 strongly enhanced LjIFS-dependent isoflavone accumulation. 3. Further reduction of competing F3H activity in the are mutant greatly increased total isoflavones.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competition with endogenous flavonol branch; product profile depends strongly on F3H activity

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation via direct promoter binding
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Flavonols and related phenylpropanoids in tomato peel
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Phenylpropanoid/flavonol biosynthesis and upstream precursor-supply genes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR peel versus flesh; ChIP-qPCR in WT peel/flesh; immunodetection of homologous protein
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyphenolic compounds involved in plant protection and nutritional quality
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SlMYB12 is predominantly expressed in tomato peel. 2. SlMYB12 binds the same promoter motifs in SlENO, SlDAHPS and SlCHS1 as AtMYB12. 3. Its expression is associated with elevated expression of genes from both primary metabolism and flavonol biosynthesis in peel.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively associated with peel flavonol/phenylpropanoid production through activation of biosynthetic and precursor-supply genes
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Peel versus flesh; 5 days after breaker
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as the functional tomato homologue of AtMYB12; not expressed in flesh, which served as a control
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Peel-specific positive regulator coordinating flavonol accumulation with precursor-supply genes
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential endogenous regulator for peel phenylpropanoid enhancement in tomato
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates SlENO, SlDAHPS and SlCHS1 expression in peel
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern: Predominantly in fruit peel, not flesh

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as part of heterologous anthocyanin-inducing TF combination
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Delila together with Rosea1 activates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes in tomato fruit. 2. Unlike AtMYB12, Del/Ros1 does not significantly activate primary metabolic genes or increase aromatic amino acids. 3. Stacking with AtMYB12 further elevates anthocyanin accumulation in Indigo fruit.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes anthocyanin accumulation, especially when combined with Ros1; effect is enhanced in the presence of AtMYB12
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Controls demand without boosting precursor supply; lower overall phenylpropanoid output than AtMYB12-based engineering
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes (late flavonoid pathway genes)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a co-engineering TF rather than the main mechanistic focus
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong activator of the anthocyanin branch but not a multi-level metabolic regulator
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in TF stacking with AtMYB12 to maximize anthocyanin-rich tomato fruit

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation as part of heterologous anthocyanin-inducing TF combination
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Rosea1 together with Delila induces anthocyanin biosynthesis in tomato fruit. 2. Del/Ros1 mainly activates anthocyanin-pathway genes and does not substantially increase primary metabolism. 3. Combining Del/Ros1 with AtMYB12 creates Indigo fruit with increased anthocyanins, flavonols and chlorogenic acid.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances anthocyanin accumulation in tomato fruit as part of the Del/Ros1 module
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Anthocyanin biosynthetic genes (late flavonoid pathway genes)
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a co-engineering TF rather than the main mechanistic focus
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong activator of anthocyanin accumulation when paired with Delila
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in combination with AtMYB12 for high-anthocyanin/high-flavonol tomato fruit
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates anthocyanin biosynthetic genes with Delila
